kt.readmeContent:
# React + TypeScript + Vite

This template provides a minimal setup to get React working in Vite with HMR and some ESLint rules.

Currently, two official plugins are available:

- [@vitejs/plugin-react](https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/blob/main/packages/plugin-react/README.md) uses [Babel](https://babeljs.io/) for Fast Refresh
- [@vitejs/plugin-react-swc](https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react-swc) uses [SWC](https://swc.rs/) for Fast Refresh

## Expanding the ESLint configuration

If you are developing a production application, we recommend updating the configuration to enable type aware lint rules:

- Configure the top-level `parserOptions` property like this:

```js
export default {
  // other rules...
  parserOptions: {
    ecmaVersion: 'latest',
    sourceType: 'module',
    project: ['./tsconfig.json', './tsconfig.node.json'],
    tsconfigRootDir: __dirname,
  },
}
```

- Replace `plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended` to `plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked` or `plugin:@typescript-eslint/strict-type-checked`
- Optionally add `plugin:@typescript-eslint/stylistic-type-checked`
- Install [eslint-plugin-react](https://github.com/jsx-eslint/eslint-plugin-react) and add `plugin:react/recommended` & `plugin:react/jsx-runtime` to the `extends` list
kt.architecture: Next.js 15 React application with TypeScript using App Router architecture. Built as a blockchain explorer for Garden Finance bridge transactions. Uses Tailwind CSS for styling, React Query for data fetching, Zustand for state management, and Motion for animations. Configured for Cloudflare deployment with standalone output. The app follows a component-based architecture with clear separation between pages, components, hooks, services, and utilities. Features responsive design with mobile/desktop variants and real-time transaction monitoring.

modules.name: App Router Pages
modules.description: Next.js App Router page components handling routing for home, search, address details, transaction details, and order details

modules.name: Common Components
modules.description: Reusable UI components including layout, navigation, search, buttons, loaders, and utility components

modules.name: Filters System
modules.description: Chain and status filtering components for transaction list filtering with dropdown selectors

modules.name: Transaction Components
modules.description: Transaction display components including order details, swap details, and transaction item renderers

modules.name: Transactions List
modules.description: Transaction list components with desktop table and mobile card layouts, includes pagination

modules.name: Page Components
modules.description: Main page-level components for Overview, Lookup, and Transaction views

modules.name: Pagination System
modules.description: Pagination components for navigating through transaction lists with skeleton loading states

modules.name: Lookup System
modules.description: Search functionality and query results display for addresses, transactions, and orders

modules.name: Order Services
modules.description: API services for fetching order data from Garden Finance orderbook

modules.name: Orderbook Service
modules.description: Core service for interacting with Garden Finance orderbook API

modules.name: State Management
modules.description: Zustand stores for assets info, viewport responsive state management

modules.name: Custom Hooks
modules.description: React hooks for orders data fetching, route parameters, time refresh, and transaction status

modules.name: Utilities
modules.description: Helper functions for formatting, parsing, date handling, and UI utilities

modules.name: Constants
modules.description: Application constants including API endpoints, global configs, and asset mappings
